mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime. Sustained low rates compress equity discount rates and reduce borrowing costs for corporations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate regimes (FEDFUNDS < 1%) are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE at 6.2%) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This triggers a negative feedback loop where weakening employment growth depresses aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,580k units) remain elevated but are beginning to decelerate from peak levels. This signals the housing cycle is maturing. Declining housing demand reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
regime_hint: housing cycle peak and early contraction
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following HOUST declines > 5% are positive in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC between HOUST changes and forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 107.18) reflects real-side capacity utilization and manufacturing health. Sustained growth in INDPRO signals robust supply-side productivity and cost control, supporting profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors recognize improving operational efficiency.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO levels or changes (Rank IC < 0.02), or INDPRO growth periods are followed by negative returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS at 568,215M) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,580k) jointly measure consumer and housing demand. When both series decline simultaneously, it signals broad-based demand weakness across the economy. This interaction triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward GDP growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month in same period
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 568,215M and HOUST at 1,580k as of 2006-07-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 2%, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 263.161) remains moderate at current levels. When CPI growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, it signals rising inflation expectations that force the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances following CPI acceleration > 0.3% MoM, or the interaction between CPI and FEDFUNDS has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 143,048k) is the primary driver of consumer income and aggregate demand. Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS signals labor market strength and rising household incomes, supporting consumer spending and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings are revised upward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS growth (Rank IC < 0.02), or months with positive PAYEMS growth are followed by negative SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 18,783.9B) growth rate is the broadest measure of economic activity. When quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized, it signals late-cycle weakness and recession risk. This regime shift produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in earnings contraction and multiple compression.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 2% annualized
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when GDPC1 growth falls below 2% annualized, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
